expectations.text: CBKSUB uses struct-based C signature (sAdamsCbksub* parameter), not Fortran-style flat params
expectations.evidence: cbksub.c uses Fortran-style flat params: void cbksub(int *id, double *time, double *par, int *npar, int *flag, int *iflag)

expectations.text: CBKSUB includes adams_c_Callback forward declaration typedef
expectations.evidence: No forward declaration typedef present. Function is declared directly with lowercase name.

expectations.text: CBKSUB uses ev_ITERATION_BEG enum constant, not a raw integer like case 4
expectations.evidence: No event-based switch at all. The function does not receive an event parameter and has no switch/case structure.

expectations.text: CBKSUB handles ev_PRIVATE_EVENT1/2 (ignores them without reading data[])
expectations.evidence: No event handling of any kind. The function has no event parameter and no switch/case.

expectations.text: CBKSUB includes slv_c_utils.h as the primary SDK header
VFOSUB uses struct-based C signature (sAdamsVforce* parameter) or correct pointer-based signature
expectations.evidence: vfosub.c uses Fortran-style flat params: void vfosub(int *id, double *time, double *par, int *npar, int *dflag, int *iflag, double *result)

expectations.text: VFOSUB includes iflag guard that skips iflag 5, 7, 9
expectations.evidence: No iflag guard present. The function always uses the cache without checking iflag.

expectations.text: VFOSUB is dflag-aware: uses cache when dflag==0, falls back to direct SYSARY when dflag==1
expectations.evidence: No dflag awareness. VFOSUB always calls get_cached_dy() regardless of dflag value.

expectations.text: Both files use c_errmes for error handling
expectations.evidence: Neither file uses c_errmes. cbksub.c uses *iflag = 1 for error signaling. vfosub.c sets *iflag = 0 unconditionally.
